content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

June 27, 1990

Siegfried H. Siefkes  
Assistant City Attorney  
City of Palms Springs  
P.O. Box 1786  
Palm Springs, CA 92263-1786

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-385

Dear Mr. Siefkes:

We have received your letter dated May 23, 1990 seeking written confirmation of the telephone advice provided to you by this agency in May, 1990 regarding the responsibilities and duties of a member of the Palm Springs Rent Review Commission under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").¹

We have also received a communication from the law firm of Shapiro, Posell & Close, a copy of which is enclosed, providing information in connection with your request.

Your letter accurately summarizes the advice that you were provided, subject to the following comment: The receipt of a deposit from the buyer, although ordinarily a part of the agreement to purchase, is not necessary to the procuring of a buyer where the contract is formed without such a deposit. That issue must be viewed in the light of the facts of each transaction.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you requested. For your convenience, a copy of your incoming letter is enclosed.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

Our File No. A-90-385
Page 2

If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5091.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Joseph Garcia
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JG:dg

Enclosure
